kuala-lumpur.title: Kuala Lumpur
kuala-lumpur.tagline: Towers, temples, and the world's best street food
kuala-lumpur.gettingThere: Fly into KLIA (KUL). KLIA Ekspres train to KL Sentral takes 28 minutes (RM55). Grab taxis widely available.
highlights: Jalan Alor night market hawker street

essentials.gettingAround: MRT, LRT, Monorail, and KTM Komuter cover most attractions. Grab for point-to-point rides RM8-25.
essentials.language: Malay, English widely spoken, Cantonese in Chinatown

penang.title: Penang
penang.tagline: UNESCO heritage streets and Malaysia's food capital
penang.gettingThere: Fly into Penang International (PEN) — daily flights from KL (45 min), Singapore, Bangkok. Alternatively, KTM train to Butterworth then 10-min ferry to George Town.
highlights: George Town UNESCO World Heritage Site
Penang street art by Ernest Zacharevic
Penang char kway teow and assam laksa

essentials.gettingAround: George Town is walkable. Grab for cross-island trips. Rental car or bicycle for the island.
connections: KL by flight 45 min or train 3.5 hrs
essentials.language: Malay, English, Hokkien Chinese widely spoken

langkawi.title: Langkawi
langkawi.tagline: Duty-free island paradise in the Andaman Sea
langkawi.gettingThere: Fly into Langkawi International (LGK) — 45-min flight from KL or Penang. Ferry from Penang takes 2.5 hours (RM80-100).
highlights: Island hopping: Pulau Dayang Bunting, Pulau Beras Basah

essentials.gettingAround: Rental car (RM70-120/day) or motorbike essential. Grab available but limited. Taxis for beach strips.

malacca.title: Malacca
malacca.tagline: Five centuries of colonial history in one city
malacca.gettingThere: Bus from KL TBS terminal takes 2 hours (RM12-15). No train or flight — bus is the only option.
highlights: St. Paul's Hill and church ruins

essentials.gettingAround: Historic city is walkable. Trishaw rides RM30-50. Grab for outer areas.
essentials.airport: Nearest is KL (2 hrs by bus)

cameron-highlands.title: Cameron Highlands
cameron-highlands.tagline: Cool tea plantations above the tropical heat
cameron-highlands.gettingThere: Bus from KL TBS takes 3.5-4 hours (RM25-35). No train or flight.
highlights: BOH Tea Plantation and factory tour
Mossy forest trekking at Gunung Brinchang

essentials.gettingAround: Local buses connect main towns. Grab limited. Taxi or rental car for plantation visits.
essentials.airport: Nearest is Ipoh (Sultan Azlan Shah Airport, IPH) 1.5 hrs

kota-kinabalu.title: Kota Kinabalu
kota-kinabalu.tagline: Gateway to Borneo's highest peak and finest reefs
kota-kinabalu.gettingThere: Fly into Kota Kinabalu International (BKI) — direct flights from KL (2 hrs), Singapore, Manila, and several Chinese cities.
highlights: Mount Kinabalu (4,095m) — highest peak in Southeast Asia
Tunku Abdul Rahman Marine Park — 5 islands, excellent snorkeling
Kinabatangan River wildlife cruise (orangutans, proboscis monkeys)

essentials.gettingAround: Grab in city. Minibuses for day trips. Day tours for Mt Kinabalu and marine park.
connections: Sandakan by flight 40 min or 5 hrs bus

sandakan.title: Sandakan
sandakan.tagline: Borneo's wildlife capital — orangutans in the wild
sandakan.gettingThere: Fly into Sandakan Airport (SDK) from Kota Kinabalu (40 min) or KL (2.5 hrs with connection). Or 5-hour bus from KK.
highlights: Kinabatangan River — one of the best wildlife corridors in Borneo
Proboscis monkeys, pygmy elephants, crocodiles on the river

essentials.gettingAround: Grab in town. Day tours and river cruises for wildlife areas.
connections: Kota Kinabalu by flight 40 min or bus 5 hrs

kuching.title: Kuching
kuching.tagline: Sarawak's charming capital — culture, wildlife, and the world's largest cave
kuching.gettingThere: Fly into Kuching International (KCH) — daily flights from KL (1.5 hrs), Singapore, Johor Bahru.
highlights: Sarawak Cultural Village — living museum of Borneo tribes
Bako National Park — proboscis monkeys and sea stacks
Sarawak Museum — best ethnographic museum in Malaysia

essentials.gettingAround: Grab in city. Boat taxis across the river. Tour operator for national parks.
connections: Mulu by flight via Miri 1 hr

mulu.title: Mulu National Park
mulu.tagline: UNESCO caves and the bat exodus spectacle
mulu.gettingThere: Fly into Mulu Airport (MZV) via Miri (MYY) — MASwings flights from Kota Kinabalu or Kuching. No road access to the park.
highlights: Deer Cave — world's largest cave passage
Bat exodus at dusk — millions of wrinkle-lipped bats
Clearwater Cave — world's longest cave in Asia

essentials.gettingAround: Everything walkable within the park. Guided cave tours required (RM30-100/cave).
essentials.airport: Mulu Airport (MZV) — small MASwings prop planes only
connections: Kuching via Miri by flight 1 hr

tioman-island.title: Tioman Island
tioman-island.tagline: Pristine jungle-covered island with world-class diving
tioman-island.gettingThere: Ferry from Mersing on mainland (1.5-2.5 hrs, RM35-45). Or ferry from Singapore via Tanah Merah (4.5 hrs). Note: no ferries during monsoon season (Nov-Feb).
highlights: Juara Beach — tranquil east coast village
Salang Beach — backpacker hub with good reefs
Snorkeling and diving among coral gardens
Jungle treks to the island interior

essentials.gettingAround: Water taxis between villages RM5-20. Sea-crossing road connects some villages. Motorbike rental on the island.
essentials.airport: Closest is Mersing jetty on mainland

perhentian-islands.title: Perhentian Islands
perhentian-islands.gettingThere: Take a bus to Kuala Besut jetty (from KL ~5-6 hrs), then speedboat to the islands (30-40 min, RM70 return). Islands closed to tourists Nov-Feb (monsoon).
highlights: Long Beach — Perhentian Kecil's main backpacker strip
Turtles, blacktip sharks, and reef fish year-round
Diving courses from RM850 (PADI Open Water)

essentials.gettingAround: Water taxis between beaches RM10-25. Most accommodation and snorkeling within walking distance.
essentials.airport: Closest is Kota Bharu (BKM) or fly to Kuala Terengganu (TGG)
connections: KL by bus 5-6 hrs then boat 30 min

taman-negara.title: Taman Negara
taman-negara.gettingThere: Bus from KL Pekeliling station to Jerantut, then local bus/boat to Kuala Tahan village (total 3-4 hrs from KL). Or direct tour bus from KL.
highlights: Night jungle walk — civets, nightjars, flying squirrels
Rapid shooting by boat on Sungai Tahan
Bukit Teresek trekking with valley views

essentials.gettingAround: Boat taxis on the river. Walking trails from Kuala Tahan village. Night hikes with guides.
essentials.airport: Nearest is KL (3-4 hrs by bus+boat)
connections: KL by bus and boat 3-4 hrs

ipoh.title: Ipoh
ipoh.tagline: Sleepy colonial city with Malaysia's best food scene
ipoh.gettingThere: KTM ETS train from KL takes 2 hours (RM28-35). Or bus from KL TBS (2.5 hrs). Easy day trip or overnight from KL.
highlights: Ipoh Old Town murals and street art
White coffee at Nam Heong or Sin Yoon Loong
Dim sum at Foh San or Ming Court

essentials.gettingAround: Old town walkable. Grab for outer areas. Bicycle rentals available.
essentials.airport: Sultan Azlan Shah Airport (IPH) — limited domestic flights
essentials.language: Malay, Cantonese Chinese widely spoken, English

george-town.title: George Town
george-town.tagline: Penang's UNESCO core — the best preserved colonial streetscape in Asia
george-town.gettingThere: Same as Penang — fly into Penang International (PEN) or arrive by train to Butterworth then ferry. George Town is the main urban area of Penang Island.
highlights: Ernest Zacharevic's iron rod street art murals
Khoo Kongsi — Malaysia's finest Hokkien clan house
Little India around Masjid Kapitan Keling
Georgetown hawker food: char kway teow, Penang laksa

essentials.gettingAround: The heritage core is fully walkable (30-min walk across). Free CAT bus along main streets.

johor-bahru.title: Johor Bahru
johor-bahru.tagline: Malaysia's southern gateway — shopping, food, and quick escapes
johor-bahru.gettingThere: Direct flights from KL (50 min), Singapore, and Jakarta. Or KTM Komuter from Singapore (Woodlands CIQ to JB Sentral). 1-hour bus from Singapore.
highlights: LEGOLAND Malaysia — largest LEGOLAND in Asia
Johor Bahru City Square shopping mall
Arulmigu Sri Rajamariamman Devasthanam temple (tallest in Malaysia)

essentials.gettingAround: Grab widely used. City is spread out — car or taxi needed for most attractions.
connections: KL by bus 4 hrs or flight 50 min
